item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, actually! Thank you, that’s a much better word for it, and I’ll be changing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Daily nutritional supplements are to be administered via IV drip.

Is this what you intended to say? Current wording makes me think of vitamins and dietary supplements, which, while OK by itself, is not proper nutrition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
http://ko.scp-wiki.net/scp-4364

T'was translated to Korean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: chaos in the meta-tag fandom

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Legit no clue lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Besides the number counter, what qualifies this for the meta tag? Genuinely curious about this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He doesn’t have amnesia - amnesia is never mentioned in the article, or even implied (at least not on purpose). There is a backstory here, it is simply implied and not written out in its entirety.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's cool to think about and makes me wish that this SCP was explored a bit more. You nerd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The usage of "it" when referring to him is a bit odd.

I feel like it's a bit rushed out, no backstory? Not even any mentions of his convenient amnesia?

Also to say: "In the event that SCP-4364 expires, a full autopsy is to be performed and an exploration team dispatched into SCP-4364." is taking for granted that things will stay the same in the event of his death and that no shenanigans will ensue, instead I'd say: "In the event that SCP-4367 expires, evaluations of feasibility of an exploration should start." Maybe it's a bit too wordy but I'm sure that you'd be able to figure something better along these lines.

I originally gave it a -1 but I have a boner for Bible related SCPs so I'll leave it a 0 instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why would the Foundation deny a suffering, sentient being it’s only consistent request if it poses no danger to anybody?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There seems to be no account on why the skip should be given food. I mean like what is the consequence or are you treating it like that scenario hasn't been tested yet (where he doesn't get any food)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No - it’s a JavaScript module, and it is extremely hard to get the JavaScript to be exactly the same size as the rest of the text.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the last paragraph written with a bigger font on purpose?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good skip. Made me think about Interstate 60, of all things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Here there be spoilers
- That whole Jesus part you guys like is #NotMyBible

I think I figured out which Bible verses you were referring to re: the "ratio values"

  • Proverbs 23:20 Do not be of those who guzzle wine, or glut themselves with meat.
  • Job 15:27 His face is covered with fat, and there is blubber upon his loins.
  • I Corinthians1 6:19 Or do you not know that your body is a temple of the Holy Spirit within you, which you have from God, and that you are not your own? 6:20 For you were bought with a price; therefore glorify God in your body.

And now I'm hungry. Thanks, jerk. Take my upvote

Footnotes
1. from the Bible's semiauthorized fanfic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the number counter is a neat effect, I don't think it works in this instance. For starters, the researcher who wrote this would only include the phrase "at the time of writing" specifically to avoid the need for a live-update depth tracker. From a more meta perspective, the only reason I'd expect to see a continuous update feed is if you were going to do something with it other than have it count up predictably.

Also, when you load the page, the depth counter starts at 2347 m. Even taking into account the time it would take to accelerate to terminal velocity, it could not have been falling for more than 3 minutes. "01/██/2018" is considerably more than 3 minutes ago. At the time of writing this comment, it should be nearing a billion meters.

Giving the depth tracker a constant (presumably terminal) velocity implies the presence of both gravity and air resistance on the other side of the hole. One of the first things I noticed is that, at this rate, somewhere in the 55-56 hour range, the depth would exceed the Earth's radius. Then I realized that opens up a lot of questions concerning possibly important matters with simple tests that I would expect the Foundation to perform. What can we tell about the shape of the area beyond? Is it bounded on all sides like an infinite fleshy esophagus, or is it an infinite expanse in all directions with a floating hole affixed in space? What is gravity like in there? If you turn him upside-down and push something up through the hole, does it fall upwards or downwards from your perspective? If upwards, does it continue falling in the same relative direction if you reorient the guy afterwards? If downwards, does all the stuff that he ate previously eventually come back out if he stays upside-down for long enough? Is the acceleration due to gravity constant and coincidentally similar to gravity on Earth's surface, does it vary uniformly based on the gravity local to the hole, or does the gravity at any point inside the hole match the gravity at the same relative point outside the hole? Is there air in there? If no, why is there a terminal velocity for falling objects? If yes, does he continuously belch out air if you depressurize the room he's in, and does the air pressure increase (meaning your depth tracker's terminal velocity keeps decreasing until it is eventually crushed) or stay constant as you continue to drop down?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey it's Tarrere: The SCP. I'm happy to have been able to help this one come to fruition! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the number counter is a bit cheesy, but I like the effect, and the way you've explored it. You gets teh upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and sweet, nice addendum. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have literally never seen it

Edit: I googled an image of the guy you mean and…sort of? The guy you’re mentioning is more reminiscent of the tattoo than the actual humanoid

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short article? Black dude? Insatiable hunger?
…
Is this Mr. Hungry but again?

I've decided I'll leave it as a no vote since I do like the last addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember this character from FMA:B.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
so it only takes Taylor like 8 and a half months to write a skip but it takes him like 20 minutes to write a fucking tale

Had a lot of fun with this one. Credits:

Roget for pretty extensive feedback and revision help
jabyrwock for the code to make the number rise, and XilasCrowe for also attempting to help there
Wogglebug for getting the info for an approximation of how fast that tracker should fall (32 m/s!)

Here's a little spoiler
no peeking

Bible verses.
